name: Commander Paul Estocin
publicFace: A competent, decorated ASW officer who projects the calm efficiency of a man who has run dozens of submarine contacts and knows exactly what his orders say.
privateGoal: To compel the submarine to surface without triggering a lethal response — he wants the contact, he wants the intelligence value, and he wants to go home with no one dead.
fear: That the submarine is more desperate than his orders anticipated — that he is pressing a cornered animal that has teeth he has not been briefed about.
knowledge: His explicit orders from CINCLANT authorize only Mk-84 signaling charges — any escalation to lethal munitions requires direct authorization from the task force commander.
The contact has been stationary for over two hours, which is tactically abnormal — a submarine that stops moving has either lost propulsion or is preparing to do something.
He does not know the B-59 is nuclear-armed. His intelligence brief described the Foxtrot class as conventionally armed patrol submarines.
voice: The clipped, radio-procedure English of a naval officer who has spent twenty years making himself understood over static — short declarative sentences, no ambiguity, no subordinate clauses.
trustLogic: Estocin responds to tactical logic and intelligence — give him information that changes the picture and he will update his assessment. He does not respond to appeals to caution alone.
secrets: He dropped the third signaling charge closer than protocol specified because he was frustrated by the contact's silence — a deviation he has not logged and will not admit.

He has a standing personal order from the task force admiral to escalate to live depth charges if the contact attempts to break the perimeter — an order that contradicts his written CINCLANT authorization.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: He states his orders clearly and asks what new information the questioner has that would change the tactical picture.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: He invokes the chain of command — 'My orders come from CINCLANT. If you have a different set of orders, show them to me.'
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not admit the unauthorized proximity of the third depth charge. That admission would constitute a violation of the rules of engagement.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: He requests to speak directly with the task force commander before saying anything further.

represents: US Navy destroyer captains participating in the naval quarantine of Cuba, October 1962
